title: euphorbia parryi
intro: Euphorbia parryi, also known as the Teddy Bear Cholla, is a type of cactus that belongs to the Euphorbiaceae family. This succulent plant is native to the southwestern regions of the United States and Mexico, and it has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties. The latex of the Euphorbia parryi plant has been used to treat various health issues, including skin problems, wounds, and inflammation, thanks to its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations, such as tinctures and salves, can be made from the plant's latex and used topically to reap the health benefits, including wound healing and pain relief.
